This months' movie: Zootopia 2 Detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde find themselves on the twisting trail of a mysterious reptile who arrives in Zootopia and turns the mammal metropolis upside down. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before.

Join Megan Snider, author and Founder of Appleseed Yoga for Kids as she delivers inspiring stories and gets the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breath work and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materia

events.title: Introduction to Complementary Food & Making Your Own Baby Food

events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: LEAP After School Program

Participants will build independence and social skills while enjoying nutritious snacks and educational workshops. The program is safe, supervised, and facilitated in partnership with North York Community House LEAP staff. events.title: Music and AI for Kids!
events.description: For ages 8-12. Join us for 4 weeks of goofy, musical fun! Our Innovator in Residence, Joel S. Silver, will show you how AI works to help you create music. events.title: After School Role-Playing Games: Mausritter
events.description: You are tiny mice seeking adventure and renown in a huge and perilous world of swords and sorcery. Will you and your companions prevail over dangerous enemies many times your size? Or will it fall to others to finish the job? In the likely event one of your mice should come to a grim demise, you can quickly create a new one and keep on playing. *Registration is required. Ages 8-12 strongly recommended.

events.title: Family Book Chat
events.description: Drop in and share a favourite book or story you’ve read together. Read a passage, tell us about your favourite character or share something new you’ve discovered. Don’t forget about non – fiction, too! Relax and Chat!

Registration includes all four dates of this series (May 13, 20, 27 & June 3).  Ages 9-12 . Registration required.

A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. Please contact us at le

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Mr. Eric's Musical Adventures
events.description: Mr. Eric's Musical Adventures is a music lesson packaged in silly fun and nonsense! Through highly engaging and catchy original songs and interactive audience participation, Mr. Eric teaches rhythm, rhyming, song writing, singing, movement, and pitch, with a big boost of vocabulary to boot! Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.title: Sphero Robotics
events.description: Join us for an introduction to coding with Sphero robots. Sphero is a ball-shaped robot that can be programmed to “drive,” “jump,” and change colours, using block coding. For ages 6-12. Registration is required. Parent/Caregiver must attend. events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program. If you or your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email accessibleservices@tpl.ca , or voicemail, 416

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Sat. Feb. 28.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Build, create and use your imagination! Join us every Saturday create with Lego, Keva Planks, Feltro and more. For children 6-12 and their caregivers.

events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' "Anne of the Library-On-The-Hill" (Plumleaf Press, August 2024), presented by the book's author Catherine Little, celebrates iconic Canadian author, Lucy Maud Montgomery, and her connection

events.description: Appleseed Yoga presents an interactive, movement-based program where stories come alive with our bodies! events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Colouring
events.description: Come to the Youth Hub with your friends and practice mindfulness with some peaceful colouring. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Game Night
events.description: Join us every Friday and play your favourite games with your friends. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Vinyl Night
events.description: Ever wonder how records compare to streaming? Explore the classics or vibe to contemporary hits - now's the time to discover the magic of analog media. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Pipe Cleaner Flower Bouquets
events.description: Do you like flowers but are very bad at keeping them alive? If this sounds like you, stop by the Hub to make long-lasting flower bouquets out of colourful pipe cleaners!  No registration required but supplies are limited. Ages 13 - 17. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.description: Come to the Thorncliffe Youth Hub for various fun activities on Friday! No registration is required. Drop-in only. events.title: Youth Hub: Board Games
events.description: Roll the dice and challenge your friends in some classic games!  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: We just got new consoles and video games! Now you can play Nintendo Switch 2 and/or PlayStation 5. Come play some games with us! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Karaoke Fridays!
events.description: Did you know we have karaoke equipment? Come sing your heart out and experience some true catharsis. A great way to end your week! If it’s not set up, just ask the librarians to grab the microphones. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Polymer Clay Beads
events.description: Create your own unique colourful beads by mixing different colours of air dry clay and shaping into beads. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Magic and Illusion Workshop
events.description: Take a journey into the mysteries of magical illusions. Local magician Ayan Deb explores the science and art of deception and illusion. In this interactive workshop, participants will enjoy and watch a series of the newest tricks in the world of magic. Participants will also get a chance to learn some simple, yet astonishing magic tricks using everyday objects.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens. Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 5th level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. events.description: Learn the basics of how to make the classic Italian dessert, Tiramisu. Chef Yumiko will provide and explain all the ingredients and take the participants through a simple step-by-step process of creating a delicious ready-to-eat dish. events.description: Learn to create your first series of basic 3D projects with the browser based software TinkerCAD! Basic computer and mouse skills required. Suitable for ages 10 and up. Equipment will be provided for this class. Registration is required. events.title: Youth Hub: DIY Keychains and Phone Charms
events.description: Design and create lovely phone charms and keychains! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Table Tennis
events.description: Kick-off your weekend with a little friendly competition. An active social hour for all ages of varying table tennis skills to come together and enjoy a dynamic match. Drop-In, No registration required.

events.title: Friday Teen Social
events.description: Drop in after school on Friday's to have fun with your friends, meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighborhood!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others! This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot. No registration required.

events.title: FRESH Movie Saturdays
events.description: This program helps people stay active, make friends, learn new skills, and have fun. Meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighbourhood! Come join us every Saturday for screenings of your favourite movies. In addition to this, enjoy cuddles with a therapy dog! Therapy dog Goose visits Fort York Branch on alternate Saturdays from 11:00AM-12:00PM. We'll be showing the following films: April 4-Honey I Shrunk the Kids April

events.title: Intro to Chess
events.description: Chess is for everyone! Learn the basics of the game including how to set up the board, how to move pieces, and how to win. All are welcome – no registration required

events.title: Innovator in Residence Kick-Off Party!
events.description: What happens when a professional composer with over 25 years in film and television music meets artificial intelligence? It's complicated. Join Joel S. Silver, TPL's Spring 2026 Innovator in Residence, for the launch of a 12-week residency exploring AI and music production at Fairview's Digital Innovation Hub. Joel has composed music for productions across CBC, Netflix, and NBC, and has spent the last decade navigating AI's growing role in the creative process — sometimes embracing it, sometimes

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Sat. Feb. 28.

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Digital Photography Basics
events.description: Explore how to handle and shoot DSLR and Mirrorless cameras while learning the basics of how to take a great photo. If you've ever wanted to up your photography game, whether you just want to get better at taking pictures with your phone, or you want to graduate to a professional DSLR or mirrorless camera, join us for a class on the basics of photography. We will go over the basic settings that go into a good photo as well as talking about camera settings, lenses, and lighting Camera equipment w

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Family Fibers
events.description: Join us on Saturday afternoon for our knitting and crochet family social. Bring your work in progress and enjoy the company of other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap pattern ideas or just relax alongside fellow fiber enthusiasts with some tea and snacks. Free yarn, clothing patterns and fabric while supplies lasts. Drop-in!  Ages 6 & up  The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you hav

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday STEAM Stations
events.description: Build, create, learn & play at various stations including Snap Circuits, LEGO, KEVA Planks, Connectors, & Colouring! This is a program for kids, teens, & families.  All are welcome!  Drop-in, no registration required. Please note: there is no program on Sun April 5, as TPL will be closed for the Easter Sunday holiday.

events.title: Sunday Family Movies
events.description: For the Month of April we'll be hosting Sunday Family Movies! Bring friends, family and a snack to wind-down while we watch movies anyone can enjoy.  Drop-in  All ages. events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony

events.title: Youth Hub: Sloppy Craft: Monoprinting and Mark Making

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.
